title: Customer Signal
claim: PMs and operators still spend hours every week reconstructing what changed, what evidence matters, and what should happen next.
source_snippet: PMs and operators still spend hours every week reconstructing what changed, what evidence matters, and what should happen next. They want clearer queue ownership, fewer manual follow-up loops, and evidence they can re...
implication: They want clearer queue ownership, fewer manual follow-up loops, and evidence they can reuse instead of restating context in every review.
unknowns: What fresh market evidence shows this problem is urgent for the target PM buyer right now?
How do current alternatives frame this workflow problem and where are their proof boundaries weaker?
followup_questions: What fresh market evidence shows this problem is urgent for the target PM buyer right now?
How do current alternatives frame this workflow problem and where are their proof boundaries weaker?
What direct PM or operator evidence quantifies the manual reconstruction burden in this workflow?
